layman_headline: Crypto market is fearful and most coins look beat up — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fear zone with a score of 28 out of 100, and Bitcoin dominance at 56.5% means money is flowing away from smaller coins toward Bitcoin. Almost every candidate on our watchlist is in a downtrend with prices below their key moving averages. While some coins like BONK and DEXE have dropped 28-31% in a week (which looks tempting for a bounce), their charts show pure selling momentum with no signs of a bottom yet. The safest approach right now is to wait for clearer reversal signals before putting money to work.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. Set price alerts for ETH at $1,800 (support level) — if it holds that level and RSI drops below 30 with a bullish candle pattern, that could be a strong entry. Don't chase anything in this fearful, downtrending environment.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling risk/reward for immediate entry. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — classic capitulation patterns that often see follow-through selling before any bounce. KAITO's RSI at 79 after a 46% weekly gain screams overextension with terrible R/R (0.46x). LDO has the best chart (+0.85 bias, Three White Soldiers) but poor R/R (0.38x to nearest resistance) and RSI at 59 after a 15% weekly move. ETH is the best candidate on a pullback to $1,800 support where RSI would likely hit oversold territory, providing a higher-quality setup with clear stop at $1,720 and target at $2,050 (roughly 3:1 R/R). High BTC dominance at 56.5% is a headwind for all alts.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues crushing alt performance — even quality alts like ETH underperform in this regime
Fear & Greed at 28 and flat-trending could deteriorate further if macro news worsens, dragging all crypto lower
No clear MACD bullish crosses or volume breakout confirmations on any candidate — all potential bounces are speculative
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with increasing volume would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index — a move below 20 (extreme fear) could signal capitulation and a broader buying opportunity
BTC dominance trend — any reversal below 55% would signal rotation back into alts and improve setups across the board
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear phase (28/100) with high BTC dominance (56.5%) punishing altcoins. BTC itself is relatively stable near $64,500 but altcoins are broadly in downtrends, making this a poor environment for swing entries without confirmed reversal signals.
context_banner: Market fear and high BTC dominance create a hostile environment for altcoin entries — capital preservation through patience is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 ('Fear') with flat weekly trend at 25.7 avg — market hasn't reached extreme fear capitulation yet, suggesting more downside possible before a durable bottom.
ETH RSI at 39 is approaching but not yet at oversold (30) — our most reliable signal source (80% win rate) suggests waiting for a deeper RSI reading before entry.
Blockworks reports 'ETH derivatives reset and the next retail trade' — suggesting leveraged positioning is clearing out, which could set up a cleaner move once support is tested.
GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline missed by US agencies creates regulatory uncertainty that could weigh on sentiment near-term, particularly for DeFi-adjacent tokens.
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — without volume confirmation, any bounce attempts have lower probability of sustaining gains.
